label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out why the employer made all those criticisms about Julia Vassilyevna's work. Let me read through the story again to understand the context. The employer is calculating how much money Julia should get. He starts by saying they agreed on thirty rubles a month, but Julia says forty. He insists on thirty and then starts deducting various days like Sundays, holidays, days when the kids were sick or she had a toothache. He subtracts money for each of these. Then he adds more deductions for breaking a teacup, the boy tearing his jacket, and shoes being stolen. He remembers giving her ten rubles before but Julia corrects him. In the end, he reduces her pay to eleven rubles but then gets angry when she thanks him, realizing he's been unfair. He ends up giving her eighty rubles instead.
